meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use an Antigravity project template and overcome a lack of portfolio. An intermediate-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends by leveraging the Antigravity project template to rapidly deploy production-grade SaaS applications. By combining Antigravity's architectural scaffolding with Claude Code's autonomous development capabilities, you transform raw ideas into live, verifiable projects in hours, building the high-impact social proof necessary to secure your first developer role.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the developer's greatest adversary. You have the skills, you have the ambition, but your GitHub profile remains a graveyard of abandoned tutorials and 'Hello World' repositories. Employers aren't looking for more theory; they are hunting for engineers who can bridge the gap between a blank IDE and a deployed, scalable application.

This is where the Antigravity project template changes the game. By automating the boilerplate of production-ready architecture, we strip away the friction that kills student projects. We aren't just writing code; we are building a professional narrative that proves you can ship, iterate, and maintain high-quality software under pressure.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish the foundational structure of your application. This ensures your project follows industry standards for folder structure, environment management, and security from line one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a full-stack SaaS project using the Antigravity base template with Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase integration.

steps.title: Define the Domain Schema
steps.description: Use Claude Code to infer your database schema based on your project requirements, ensuring type safety across the entire stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a task management SaaS including User, Project, and Task models with appropriate relationships.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate prisma schema and run migrations'

steps.title: Implement Core Business Logic
steps.description: Translate user requirements into functional backend services using Claude Code to handle the boilerplate API routes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement the core CRUD operations for tasks, ensuring authentication is enforced via middleware.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create API routes for CRUD operations'

steps.title: Integrate UI Components
steps.description: Rapidly build the frontend using Antigravity's component library to ensure a professional, responsive user interface.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a dashboard layout using Antigravity UI components that displays user-specific tasks and summary stats.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Build dashboard component with responsive grid'

steps.title: Automated Testing & Deployment
steps.description: Ensure your project is production-ready by generating automated tests and configuring the deployment pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write unit tests for the task service and generate a Vercel configuration file for deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate vitest suite and configure deployment'

pro_tips: Never commit API keys; use environment variables and ensure .env is in your .gitignore before the first push.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that solves a specific user pain point rather than building a bloated, generic CRUD app.
Write a detailed README that explains not just the 'what' of your app, but the 'why' behind your architectural decisions.
Use Claude Code to refactor your own code after finishing a module; ask it specifically to 'optimize for readability and performance'.
Deploy early and often; a live URL is worth ten thousand lines of local code during a portfolio review.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, perform a code audit using Claude Code to identify areas for optimization, then write a case study blog post detailing the challenges you solved to add to your portfolio site.
content.conversion_block: You now have a blueprint to bypass the entry-level noise and showcase real, production-grade engineering. Don't let your skills stay hidden behind a wall of tutorials. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
